# VideoFrame Extractor

> A free, browser-based tool to extract frames from any video — no uploads, 100% private.

VideoFrame Extractor is a client-side web application that captures individual frames from video files using the HTML5 Canvas API. All processing happens entirely on the user's device. Videos are never sent to any server.

## What It Does

Given a video file (or a CORS-enabled video URL), VideoFrame Extractor lets users:

- Extract frames at specific timestamps, intervals, or counts
- Automatically detect scene changes in a video
- Identify moments when a camera stops after motion (property walkthrough mode)
- Export individual frames as JPEG, PNG, or WebP images
- Batch-download all extracted frames as a ZIP archive
- Resize output frames to multiple standard resolutions

## Extraction Modes

1. **By Count** — Extract exactly N frames, evenly distributed across the full video duration (or a specified time range).
2. **By Interval** — Extract one frame every N seconds throughout the video.
3. **Manual Timestamps** — User provides a comma-separated list of times in seconds; one frame is extracted per timestamp.
4. **Scene Change Detection** — A pixel-difference algorithm samples frames at a configurable rate and flags frames where the visual difference exceeds a threshold. Configurable parameters: sample rate, difference threshold, minimum gap between detected scenes.
5. **Property Walkthrough** — Designed for real estate / inspection videos. Detects camera motion and captures a frame only after motion stops and the scene has settled for a configurable duration. Parameters: sample rate, motion threshold, settle time, minimum gap between captures.

## Supported Input Formats

MP4, WebM, MOV, MKV, AVI — any format supported by the user's browser `<video>` element.

Input can be:
- A local file (uploaded via drag-and-drop or file picker)
- A remote video URL (must have CORS `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` headers)

## Output Options

- **Format**: JPEG (default, smallest size), PNG (lossless), WebP (modern format)
- **Quality**: 10–100% (applies to JPEG and WebP)
- **Resize**: Original resolution, 4K (3840px wide), FHD 1080p, HD 720p, 480p, 360p
- **Download**: Individual frames or all frames bundled as a ZIP file

## Privacy & Security

- No server-side processing. The app has no backend.
- No analytics, tracking pixels, or third-party scripts.
- All video data stays on the user's device.
- Works fully offline once loaded.

## Technology Stack

- React 19, Vite 8, Tailwind CSS 4
- HTML5 Canvas API for frame capture
- JSZip for ZIP archive generation
- FileSaver.js for browser downloads
- Deployed on Vercel

## Use Cases

- Extracting thumbnails from product demo videos
- Capturing frames from real estate walkthrough videos for property listings
- Creating image datasets from video footage
- Reviewing video content frame-by-frame
- Generating storyboards from video
- Extracting key moments from recorded meetings or lectures

## URL

https://videoextract.thecfguy.com/
